_instructions: Copy this file to knowledge/game-engines/<kebab-case-engine-name>.json and fill in all fields.
Remove all _instructions and _comment fields before submitting.
The engine field must exactly match what game files will use in their engine field.
Only add keys that have a well-documented, widely-applicable default — avoid game-specific values here.
Every recommended value must have a source citation in the sources array.
One file per MAJOR engine version only (e.g. 'unreal-engine-4.json', 'unreal-engine-5.json'). Never create separate files for minor versions.
Minor-version differences (e.g. UE4 4.25 vs 4.27 render path changes) belong in the Tier 1 game file's engine_overrides section, not here.
Use fallback_for to cover future or related major versions until a dedicated file exists for them.
engine: REQUIRED — exact engine name, e.g. 'Unreal Engine 5'. Must match what game files reference.
version_range: REQUIRED — major version string this file covers, e.g. '5.x'. Always use major-only granularity (e.g. '4.x', '5.x'). Minor-version quirks belong in Tier 1 game files.
profile_version: REQUIRED — semantic version of this profile, e.g. '1.0.0'. Increment minor for new keys or corrections, patch for typo/source fixes.
updated: REQUIRED — ISO 8601 date of last meaningful update, e.g. '2026-04-28'
fallback_for: OPTIONAL — list engine names this file covers as a fallback when no dedicated file exists for them.
REMOVE this field if not applicable.
Resolution rule: a dedicated exact-match file always wins. This field is only consulted when no exact file exists.

detection_signatures._comment: Used by ReFrame to identify this engine from file names and content. Remove _comment.
file_names: REQUIRED — config file names that are characteristic of this engine, e.g. 'GameUserSettings.ini'
section_headers: OPTIONAL — INI section headers that identify this engine, e.g. '[/Script/Engine.GameUserSettings]'
key_prefixes: OPTIONAL — key name prefixes that identify this engine, e.g. 'sg.', 'r.'

config_file_locations._comment: Typical config directory path template. Use env var placeholders. Remove _comment.

keys._comment: One entry per engine-default key. Only include keys with a widely-applicable default. Remove _comment.
keys.section: OPTIONAL — section header the key belongs in
keys.file: REQUIRED — which config file this key belongs in, e.g. 'GameUserSettings.ini'
keys.effect: REQUIRED — plain English description of what this key controls

recommendations.performance: REQUIRED — engine default recommendation for a performance goal
recommendations.balanced: REQUIRED — engine default recommendation for a balanced goal
recommendations.quality: REQUIRED — engine default recommendation for a quality goal

keys.notes: OPTIONAL — important caveats. In particular: note any known games that override this key so contributors know to check game files first.

sources._comment: REQUIRED — at least one entry. Remove _comment field. Accepted types: wiki, official, community, editorial.
sources.type: REQUIRED — one of: wiki | official | community | editorial
sources.label: REQUIRED — human-readable label, e.g. 'Unreal Engine Documentation — Scalability Reference'
